pages.name: Case Studies
pages.instruction_text:
We need info on the 
  • Names of Projects for case studies
  • Detailed narratives for 5–6 featured projects including "Before/After" descriptions.
  • The first 2 photo upload fields are for the slider feature. Make sure the main photo is the same angle and area so the slider photo aligns.
    Use the example on the lower part of the homepage at https://elevatedfireplace.com/ for a reference.
  • Each project has fields for you to upload a Before and After video, along with a field to upload any other videos you have for the project.

fields.instruction_text: ie: 2180 Palm Canyon Drive

fields.name: Please provide a description of the project or work you are submitting.
fields.instruction_text: Provide a detailed description that highlights the key aspects of your project.
values:
This Palm Springs project transforms a residential backyard into a modern desert oasis, seamlessly blending mid-century modern architecture with functional outdoor living spaces. The clean, geometric layout is defined by porcelain pavers and acid wash concrete, creating distinct zones for relaxation and entertainment. This design balances low-maintenance desert materials with lush functional areas.

The renovation introduces multiple destination spots within the backyard. The existing curved pool and spa are refreshed with acid-washed concrete coping and surrounded by porcelain paver decking, perfect for sunbathing. A dedicated seating area features a large Corten steel fire bowl, while an outdoor shower area is screened by breeze block.

The planting strategy combines existing mature greenery with new, structurally significant desert flora. Architectural succulents like Yucca rostrata, Madagascar Palms, and an Olive tree are incorporated into the design.

ie: 2180 Palm Canyon Drive

fields.name: Please provide a description of the project or work you are submitting.
fields.instruction_text: Provide a detailed description that highlights the key aspects of your project.
values:
The primary objective of this project was to undertake a comprehensive landscape renovation, transforming the property into a functional and aesthetically harmonious “Desert Modernist” outdoor living space.
The backyard underwent a redesign to serve as an extension of the home, incorporating a comprehensive outdoor kitchen (equipped with a grill, pizza oven, and sink), a pool and spa area, and multiple distinct seating and dining zones. The design incorporates iconic desert modern elements, notably a double-sided fireplace with breeze block accents and masonry benches with a stucco finish.
The front yard renovation involved the installation of a new 5-foot-tall stuccoed block wall and a contemporary entry gate to enhance privacy and delineate the property boundary.
Implementing a Desert-Adapted Landscape: The plant selection prioritizes drought-resistant, architectural species such as Yucca Rostrata, Hercules Aloe, and Blue Barrel Cactus, all situated within a structured ground cover of multi-colored crushed rock.
This transformation expanded the usable living space and enhanced the privacy, functionality, and aesthetic value of the property.

fields.name: Please provide a description of the project or work you are submitting.
fields.instruction_text: Provide a detailed description that highlights the key aspects of your project.
values:
The objective of this project was a complete landscape renovation for a residence in Indian Wells. The project aimed to transform the property's backyard into a modern outdoor living and entertainment space.

The project included significant updates to the pool and spa and overall hardscape.
Entertainment Zones were designed including a fire pit seating area and a 4K covered patio.

The design utilized a low-maintenance palette of desert-adapted plants, such as Yucca rostrata, various cacti (San Pedro, Golden Barrel, Totem Pole), and Agaves (Artichoke, Blue Glow).
To achieve a clean, contemporary look, the renovation combined sand-finish concrete and a new seat wall with artificial turf segments and "Chamois Beige" crushed rock mulch.

Ultimately, the renovation sought to balance aesthetic appeal with high functionality, providing the homeowners with multiple outdoor areas for relaxation and social gatherings

ie: 2180 Palm Canyon Drive

fields.name: Please provide a description of the project or work you are submitting.
fields.instruction_text: Provide a detailed description that highlights the key aspects of your project.
values:
The primary objective of this project was to create a harmonious and multi-functional outdoor space for relaxation and entertainment. The design direction is inspired by the “Palm Springs Modern” aesthetic, characterized by a neutral palette of whites and greys in the hardscape, which provides a sophisticated backdrop for vibrant pops of color. The project incorporates architectural elements like mid-century-inspired breeze block walls to define distinct “rooms” within the yard while maintaining an open and airy atmosphere.

The site features high-quality materials, including 24”x48” Porcelain Pavers and natural grey concrete with a sand finish. The design centers around an existing pool and spa, enhanced with a raised bond beam featuring mitered edge stone coping and new tile. Outdoor living amenities include a BBQ island with a waterfall counter and decorative blue-and-white wave tiling, an Alumawood shade structure equipped with recessed lighting, ceiling fans, and a misting system for year-round comfort. A Lumacast fire pit with a white aesthetic complements the surrounding pavers.

The plant palette consists of drought-tolerant species such as Golden Barrel Cactus, Agave (Blue Flame and Octopus), Totem Pole Cactus, and Date Palms. These are balanced with artificial turf areas used for lawn games and visual softening.

Architectural details include a modern 5-foot-high breeze block wall that serves as functional dividers and privacy screens. Decorative metal screens are used to conceal utility areas like air conditioning units while adding a decorative element.

The design incorporates a layered lighting scheme featuring string lights on poles, plant uplighting, and integrated lighting on decorative panels and the shade structure.

fields.name: Please provide a description of the project or work you are submitting.
fields.instruction_text: Provide a detailed description that highlights the key aspects of your project.
values:
The objective of this project is to create a private, mid-century modern backyard that seamlessly integrates functional outdoor living spaces with the home's architecture. The design direction prioritizes clean lines, geometric patterns, and drought-tolerant landscaping to complement the Palm Springs environment while providing high-end amenities.

Key Aspects of the Project include a new Ficus hedge around the property boundaries to create a secluded environment. The yard is anchored by a large, renovated curved swimming pool and a new integrated spa that features the use of acid washed concrete. A centerpiece of the backyard is a custom outdoor shower featuring a bold mid-century geometric tile pattern and a wooden bench. A standalone concrete fire bowl is positioned in a gravel bed, providing a social focal point.

The "Planting Schedule" focuses on low-maintenance, water-wise plants including various species of Agave, Yucca, and Golden Barrel cactus. These are balanced by large expanses of synthetic turf (lawn) to provide greenery without the high water demands of natural grass.

The installation includes strategically placed landscape lighting, such as path lights and spotlights directed at architectural plants like the Yuccas and Ficus, extending the usability of the space into the evening.

ie: 2180 Palm Canyon Drive

fields.name: Please provide a description of the project or work you are submitting.
fields.instruction_text: Provide a detailed description that highlights the key aspects of your project.
values:
The primary objective of this Palm Springs project was to transform the residential outdoor space into a modern, low-maintenance environment that prioritizes recreation, relaxation, and desert-appropriate aesthetics.

The design incorporated clean geometric lines and a palette of drought-tolerant plants. A significant emphasis was placed on creating multi-functional spaces, such as a putting green with multiple holes and flags, and a lounge centered around a 60” Lumacast fire feature. To minimize water usage while maintaining a vibrant look, the plan utilized artificial turf and crushed rock blends instead of traditional grass.

The entire setting is framed by desert-inspired plants and distant mountain views, creating a luxurious and serene atmosphere.
